chapters.notes: Logos prologue, life/light/darkness, John witness, Word embodied and tenting among us, grace/truth, Lamb of God, first disciples, come-and-see/remain motif, Simon/Peter, Philip/Nathanael, Son of Humanity heaven-opened imagery.
Cana wedding sign, water into wine, first sign revealing glory, disciples trust, temple action, Father's house, temple/body saying, sign-trust distinction.

Nicodemus by night, born from above/anew, water and Spirit, Spirit/wind wordplay, lifted Son of Humanity, Divine love for world, saving/healing rather than condemning world, light/judgment, John bridegroom witness, Spirit without measure.

Samaritan woman, living water, truth without shame reading, worship in Spirit and truth, ego eimi self-disclosure, woman as city witness, Samaritan trust, Savior of the world, royal official's son and word-trust.
Pool healing with textual variant bracketed, Sabbath controversy, Father/Son work, life/judgment, witnesses, Scripture/Moses testimony.
Feeding sign, walking on sea with ego eimi, bread from heaven discourse, bread of life, hard saying, Spirit/life, Peter confession, Judas handing-over.
Booths feast, hiddenness/time, temple teaching, just judgment, origin dispute, officers, living water at feast, Spirit note, division, Nicodemus, and 7:53 textual transition.

Pericope adulterae bracketed as later textual tradition, light of world, testimony/Father, lifted Son of Humanity, truth/free, ancestry dispute, adversarial father imagery, glory, Abraham, and before Abraham I am.
Man born blind, anti-victim-blame opening, clay/washing at Siloam/Sent, Sabbath dispute, parents and synagogue fear, expulsion, Son of Humanity, sight/judgment reversal.
Sheepfold, door, good shepherd, abundant life, one flock/one shepherd, Dedication feast, Father/Son oneness, no one seizes from the hand, Psalm divine-ones quotation, Jordan return.
Lazarus, Martha/Mary, resurrection and life I am, Jesus weeping, Lazarus come out, loose him and let him go, council plot, Caiaphas prophecy, scattered children gathered into one.
Mary anointing/fragrance, Lazarus witness, Jerusalem entry, Greeks seeking Jesus, grain of wheat, life/soul surrender, Father voice, lifted up drawing all, final public light appeal.

translation_notes: John should preserve its distinct mystical and theological vocabulary while keeping the restored layer source-language-driven and cautious.
Logos should be rendered as Word, with glossary support for living intelligibility/divine expression in companion layers, not over-expanded in the restored verse text.
Zoe should be rendered as life, with mystical companion notes for divine/living life where context supports.
Phos and skotia should preserve light/darkness imagery without flattening into moral caricature.
Kosmos should be rendered as world/cosmos according to context, preserving both beloved creation and resistant world-order tensions.
Anothen in John 3 should preserve born from above/anew ambiguity.
Pneuma should preserve Spirit/wind/breath resonances where the text itself plays on them.
Ego eimi should be preserved as I am where emphatic or theologically loaded.
Meno should be rendered remain/abide according to context, preserving relational indwelling.
Parakletos should be treated carefully as Advocate/Helper/Paraclete with glossary support.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
Verse-number alignment must remain intact in both restored and mystical companion files.
John 1-4 establishes the Johannine core: Word, life, light, darkness, world, witness, remain, born from above/anew, Spirit/wind, living water, worship in Spirit and truth, and Savior of the world.

John 5-8 establishes Father/Son work, life/judgment, sign-to-bread discourse, Spirit/life, living water from within, light of world, truth/free, and the major ego eimi climax before Abraham. Later textual traditions at John 5:4 and 7:53-8:11 are bracketed honestly.

John 9-12 establishes sight/judgment reversal, anti-victim-blame, Good Shepherd/one flock, Father-Son oneness, Lazarus as resurrection-life sign, Mary anointing, Greeks seeking Jesus, grain-of-wheat fruitfulness, lifted-up drawing, and final public light appeal.
next_actions: Continue John 13-17: foot washing, new commandment, farewell discourse, way/truth/life, Paraclete/Advocate, vine and branches, hatred of world-order, Spirit of truth, grief to joy, high-priestly prayer, oneness language.

Add glossary entries or reuse shared hooks: logos, zoe, phos, skotia, kosmos, anothen, pneuma, hydor zon, ego eimi, meno, martyria, doxa, charis, aletheia, sarx, skenoo, monogenes, parakletos, artos tes zoes, huios tou anthropou, poimen kalos, and zelos/doxa.
